title: Boat Rental in Barbados: Tips for Chartering in 2026
html:
When you rent a boat in Barbados, Bridgetown is the main departure point and prices range from 250 to 5,000 EUR per day. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators for a smooth booking experience.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Barbados?

Shared tours start at 100 EUR per person, while private half-day motorboat hire begins around 350 EUR. A full-day catamaran charter for up to 12 guests costs about 800 EUR, and a crewed luxury yacht with skipper and host ranges from 2,000 to 5,000 EUR per day. For the latest prices, compare verified boat listings on yacht charter Barbados options available through BoatHire24.

Where to set off in Barbados

Several well-equipped marinas and natural harbours serve as ideal departure points. Bridgetown Port offers easy access to the west and south coasts. Port St. Charles Marina in the north provides a sheltered basin, while Port Ferdinand Marina on the west coast is a modern facility with full services. Oistins Bay on the south coast is a lively fishing village and a good spot for smaller boat hires. Carlisle Bay, close to Bridgetown, is perfect for anchoring and watersports. For island day trips, set course for Sandy Lane Bay or the calm waters off Speightstown.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motor yacht, ideal for cruising the west coast, up to 12 guests, from 1,500 EUR per day. The Princess F70 is a popular choice; see the Princess F70 listing for details.
  2. Catamaran, best for sunset sails and snorkelling trips with groups, 8, 16 guests, from 800 EUR per day.
  3. Sports boat, for waterskiing and wakeboarding, up to 6 guests, from 400 EUR half-day.
  4. Sailing yacht, for a classic bluewater experience, up to 8 guests, from 900 EUR per day.
  5. RIB tender, for quick island hops, up to 5 guests, from 250 EUR half-day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Barbados?

No licence is required for most bareboat rentals if the vessel is under a certain size and you have basic boating experience. However, many operators conduct a brief sea trial to assess competence. For larger motor yachts and catamarans, a valid International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or equivalent may be requested. Always confirm with the operator before booking, as requirements vary.

A local tip: book your boat for a full day to explore the unspoilt east coast, where the Atlantic swell creates dramatic surf and fewer tourists venture. The journey from Bridgetown to Bathsheba takes about 45 minutes by boat.

At a glance

  • Price range: 100, 5,000 EUR per person or per vessel
  • Peak months: December to April
  • Trip length: half-day (4 hours) to full-day (8 hours)
  • Typical guest capacity: 4, 12 for private charters
  • Currency: EUR (most operators quote in EUR for international bookings)

Questions about boat rental in Barbados

Can I rent a boat for just a few hours?

Yes, many operators offer half-day charters of four hours, particularly for sports boats and small motorboats.

What is the best time of year to rent a boat in Barbados?

The dry season from December to April offers the most reliable conditions, with steady trade winds and minimal rainfall.

Are there any age restrictions for driving a rental boat?

Typically, you must be at least 18 years old and hold a valid driving licence or ICC, but confirm with the individual operator.

What should I bring on a boat charter in Barbados?

Pack sunscreen, a hat, swimwear, a light jacket for windy conditions, and cash for any harbour fees or tips.

Whether you choose a self-drive sports boat or a fully crewed luxury yacht, booking through BoatHire24 ensures transparent pricing and verified operators. Boat owners in Barbados can also list your boat on the platform to reach a global audience. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Barbados or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Barbados and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
